summary: This is a very brief news update post where Joel links to a report and photos from a Fog Creek Open House event. He also highlights a fellow attendee's website, Deadprogrammer's Cafe, using it to illustrate his observation about the density of interesting experiences in New York City compared to smaller cities.
key_points: Fog Creek held an open house event that was documented by attendee JD with photos and a report
Joel highlights Deadprogrammer's Cafe as a site whose photonarratives capture the richness of daily life in New York City
Joel reiterates his belief that New York provides an extraordinary density of noteworthy everyday experiences that would be remarkable elsewhere
money_quotes: New York is the kind of place where ten things happen to you every day on the way to the subway that would have qualified as interesting dinner conversation in Bloomington, Indiana, and you don't pay them any notice.
key_insight: New York City offers such a density of interesting daily experiences that what would be remarkable elsewhere goes entirely unnoticed by its residents.
